retrospective.harder_than_needed: The stories.json scope note said MarmotContext.tsx's diff must stay 'confined to the approveJoinRequest call site', but the project's own no-jsdom/no-@testing-library/renderHook testing convention (pendingRequestsSection.test.ts's own comment: MarmotContext 'is difficult to unit test' for this reason) means a useCallback-wrapped closure cannot be invoked by any test without rendering React. Satisfying VQ-S2-004/009/010/011's requirement for real spy-based behavioral tests (not source-inspection alone) on AC-USAGE-1/2/3 required extracting approveJoinRequest's full body into a new exported, dependency-injected function (approveJoinRequestImpl) placed just before MarmotProvider, with the useCallback reduced to a thin resolver/delegator. This was a judgment call between the literal scope wording and the verification set's actual testing bar, resolved by following the codebase's own established in-file extraction pattern already used for fireAutoCommit/resolveAndApplyPairingAdmissionDigest — not a new pattern invented for this story. Flagged explicitly as VQ-S2-012 for verifier confirmation rather than silently deviating.
